name: 30 · xArm Joint Move (ROS)
description:
End-to-end ROS-backend demo against a SIMULATED UFACTORY xArm 6 (xarm_ros2, ROS 2 Jazzy) running headless in ros2_control fake-hardware mode + MoveIt, reachable over rosbridge. A four-step net plans a joint move, executes it, and reads back the resulting joint state — the next rung up from the turtlesim demos (28/29), exercising the data shapes the flat turtle never did: ARRAY fields, in both request authoring and output-port derivation, plus a non-bundled custom message package (`xarm_msgs`).

  Start → plan (call_service /xarm_joint_plan, xarm_msgs/srv/PlanJoint, {target:[0,-0.3,-0.3,0,0.6,0]}) → execute (call_service /xarm_exec_plan, xarm_msgs/srv/PlanExec, {wait:true}) → read_state (await_topic /joint_states, sensor_msgs/msg/JointState) → End.

The `plan` request carries `target`, a six-element float64[] of joint angles in radians — the first ROS service request the platform authors with an array. The `read_state` step derives its output port from JointState, whose `name`/`position`/`velocity`/`effort` are arrays — the first array OUTPUT fields the platform derives from a live ROS message; each maps to a Json port field with a JSON-Schema array override. Each step embeds `interface_typedefs` (the runner's self-reported catalog entry) so the deriver shapes the ports live for this non-bundled robot, no bundled snapshot needed. End maps `plan_ok = {{ plan.success }}`, `exec_ok = {{ execute.success }}`, `joint_names = {{ read_state.name }}`, `positions = {{ read_state.position }}` onto the instance result.

Each ROS AutomatedStep is deployed to the `xarm_fleet` presence pool (seeded from demos/resources/xarm_fleet.json) AND declares the placement Requirement `ros.robot_model == xarm6` over the shared `ros` capability type (seeded from demos/capability-types/ros.json — the same type the turtle uses, the xArm just advertises a different robot_model). At claim time the compiler injects the Requirement into the claim payload; the presence pool's `t_grant` guard admits ONLY a runner whose advertised caps satisfy it. The ROS connection is runner-local — the runner advertises a reachable rosbridge and its executor is configured with the WebSocket URL (`EXECUTOR_ROS__WS_URL`); there is no workspace resource binding.

LIVE ONLY: this demo can only be RUN after `just dev xarm-up`, which builds the headless xArm fake-mode image, runs it on a slot-aware host port (service port + 42), and enrolls an `xarm_fleet` runner advertising `ros.robot_model=xarm6, ros.ros_distro=jazzy` whose executor points `EXECUTOR_ROS__WS_URL` at that bridge. Without xarm-up there is no capacity and the instance parks at the first claim. A valid target plans and executes, so `plan_ok` and `exec_ok` are both true and `positions` carries the six post-move joint angles.
